steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best places the 1821 Declaration in the context of Valle's changing political beliefs. The answer must connect three things: his initial stance against independence, what changed his mind, and his authorship of the declaration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Fails to establish Valle's prior opposition to independence, so there is no 'change' to contextualize. ✗
  • Choice B — Describes Valle as 'long an opponent of Central American independence,' identifies the 1820 revolt as what changed his mind, and states he 'wrote the 1821 declaration'—all three required pieces. ✓
  • Choice C — Bypasses Valle's beliefs entirely and does not show any shift in his views. ✗
  • Choice D — Misidentifies when or why Valle changed his mind and does not credit him with writing the declaration. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
